sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: |‘In storm prep mode’: Virginia power companies prepare for ice storm outages
feed.summary: |Virginia electric companies, including Rappahannock Electric Cooperative and Dominion Energy, are preparing for potential widespread power outages due to an impending ice storm. They have mobilized additional crews and requested mutual aid from other states to respond to the threat. Residents are advised to stay away from downed power lines, and restoration efforts will prioritize critical infrastructure like hospitals and water treatment plants.

feed.title: Infleqtion Appoints CFO Eric Bjornholt To Board Ahead Of Public Listing
feed.summary: Infleqtion has appointed J. Eric Bjornholt, CFO of Microchip Technology Inc., to its Board of Directors, a strategic move as the company prepares for a public listing in September 2025 following a business combination with Churchill Capital Corp X. Bjornholt, with over 30 years of financial experience, will also chair the Board's Audit Committee, bringing significant public-company finance and governance expertise to the quantum technology firm. Infleqtion's diverse product portfolio, including quantum computers and precision sensors, is already deployed with clients like the U.S. Department of War and NASA.

feed.title: Why Is Pfizer Stock Rising Tuesday?
feed.summary: Pfizer stock is rising due to Cantor Fitzgerald increasing its price forecast for the company from $24 to $27. Recent activities supporting Pfizer include a licensing agreement with Novavax Inc. for Matrix-M adjuvant technology and its drug sunitinib receiving FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation in combination with Cogent Biosciences' bezuclastinib. The company's CEO also commented on global health research funding at the World Economic Forum.

feed.title: Final Trades: AbbVie, SLB, Amphenol and the IYH
feed.summary: The article features the "Halftime Report" segment where the Investment Committee provides their top stock picks for the second half of the year. The recommended stocks include AbbVie, SLB, Amphenol, and the IYH. The content also features several other unrelated video clips on various business and economic topics.

feed.title: International Paper Set to Report Q4 Earnings: What's in Store?
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) is scheduled to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results on January 29, 2026. The Zacks Consensus Estimate projects a significant increase in revenues to $5.88 billion and earnings of 28 cents per share, largely due to the acquisition of DS Smith. However, the Zacks Model does not predict an earnings beat, as IP currently has an Earnings ESP of -10.18% and a Zacks Rank of 5 (Strong Sell), and has historically missed earnings estimates in three of the last four quarters.

feed.title: Bernstein Initiates Hewlett Packard Enterprise(HPE.US) With Hold Rating, Announces Target Price $24
feed.summary: Bernstein analyst Mark Newman has initiated coverage on Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE.US) with a 'Hold' rating. The target price for HPE has been set at $24. Newman has a 60% success rate and an average return of 23.0% over the past year according to TipRanks data.

feed.title: Seagate (STX) is Facing a Shortage That Can't Be Met, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Seagate Technology Holdings plc (NASDAQ:STX) as a company facing an unmet product shortage, contributing to its significant stock performance. The shares of the storage device manufacturer are up 256% over the past year. Analysts from BofA and Rosenblatt have raised their price targets for STX, citing strong demand for high-density memory devices and the potential of Seagate's heat-assisted recording technology.

feed.title: Micron Technology stock hits all-time high at $412.48
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc.'s stock reached an all-time high of $412.48, reflecting a remarkable 366.15% 1-year change driven by strong investor confidence and growth in the semiconductor industry. The company's strategic initiatives, including a planned investment in NAND flash memory production in Singapore, and positive analyst outlooks, with several firms raising price targets, contribute to its robust market performance. Micron boasts a market capitalization of $463.39 billion and impressive revenue growth of 45.43%.

feed.title: Halliburton director Banks sells $88,854 in stock
feed.summary: Halliburton director Margaret Katherine Banks sold 2,600 shares of company stock for a total of $88,854. This transaction comes as Halliburton's stock shows strong momentum, trading near its 52-week high, and the company recently reported Q4 2025 earnings that exceeded analyst expectations. Analysts have adjusted price targets for HAL based on these positive results and favorable margin outlook.

feed.title: INTEGER HOLDINGS CORPORATION (NYSE: ITGR) SHAREHOLDER ALERT
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has issued a shareholder alert for Integer Holdings Corporation (NYSE: ITGR) investors regarding an upcoming deadline in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased Integer common stock between July 25, 2024, and October 22, 2025, and suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm, with the lead plaintiff deadline set for February 9, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Integer made misrepresentations about its market position and demand for its electrophysiology devices.

feed.title: How 2026 AI Spending Forecasts Could Reshape Big Tech Stock Performance
feed.summary: This article discusses how capital expenditure projections for AI spending by hyperscalers like Microsoft, Meta, Alphabet, and Amazon will be crucial for their stock performance in 2026. While financial results for the past quarter might meet expectations, the market's focus will primarily be on the future capex guidance for AI development. Analyst David Sekera suggests that if these spending forecasts do not continue to increase, investors in AI stocks, many of which are already considered overvalued, could face disappointment.

feed.title: F5, INC. (NASDAQ: FFIV) SHAREHOLDER ALERT Bernstein
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP has issued a shareholder alert for investors of F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV), reminding them of an upcoming deadline in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased F5 shares between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, and experienced losses, are encouraged to contact the firm for legal options and to consider serving as lead plaintiff by February 17, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that F5 and its senior officers made misrepresentations regarding the company's security capabilities.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Likely To Report Higher Q4 Revenue; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ: TXN) is expected to announce higher Q4 revenue and earnings. Analysts predict $1.31 EPS and $4.44 billion in revenue. Several analysts have revised their price targets and ratings for TXN ahead of the earnings call on January 27th.

feed.title: Akamai Is Recognized as a 2026 Gartner® Peer Insights™ Customers’ Choice for Network Security Microsegmentation
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies, Inc. has been named a Customers’ Choice in the January 2026 Gartner Peer Insights “Voice of the Customer” for Network Security Microsegmentation, achieving a 99% recommendation rate. This recognition highlights Akamai's strong customer focus and user experience, with Gartner noting the increasing importance of microsegmentation in network security. The report includes positive testimonials from customers across various industries praising the Guardicore experience, product reliability, and excellent customer service.

feed.title: Magnolia Oil raised, Matador Resources cut at Wells Fargo amid uncertain oil macro
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has revised analyst ratings for several oil companies, upgrading Magnolia Oil & Gas to Overweight from Equal Weight due to its strong financial position and potential for shareholder returns. Conversely, Matador Resources was downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight despite increasing production, with concerns cited about its valuation and capital expenditures. The bank also maintained its Equal Weight rating on Chord Energy, emphasizing its unique business model.

feed.title: Boston Scientific completes Nalu Medical acquisition
feed.summary: Boston Scientific has completed its acquisition of Nalu Medical, expanding its chronic pain portfolio with Nalu's innovative neurostimulation platform. This deal, valued at an upfront cash payment of $533 million for the remaining equity, follows Boston Scientific's initial strategic investment in Nalu in 2017. The Nalu system offers a battery-free, miniaturized implantable pulse generator for treating chronic neuropathic pain, and Boston Scientific anticipates over $60 million in revenue from Nalu in 2025.

feed.title: PREVIEW: Texas Instruments seen reporting profit dip as costs rise
feed.summary: Texas Instruments is expected to report a decline in profit as the company faces increasing costs. This preview highlights the financial outlook for the semiconductor giant according to analyst expectations. The report suggests that the company's Q1 performance will be impacted by rising operational expenses.

feed.title: EQT CEO shows how natural gas keeps flowing as snowstorm tests Pennsylvania producers
feed.summary: EQT CEO Toby Z. Rice demonstrated how natural gas producers maintain operations during extreme winter weather by taking X users behind the scenes at an EQT well pad during a recent snowstorm. Despite rising prices and potential production threats, Pennsylvania's largest natural gas producers, including EQT, reported smooth operations. This highlights the resilience of natural gas supply even as severe weather tests energy infrastructure.

feed.title: Pacific Imperial Mines options Fenton property, British Columbia
feed.summary: Pacific Imperial Mines Inc. has entered into an option agreement with Hudbay Minerals Inc. to acquire a 100% interest in the Fenton property in British Columbia. To exercise the option, Pacific Imperial must incur C$5,250,000 in exploration expenditures, make initial cash payments, and make additional cash or share payments over six years. Upon exercise, Hudbay will be granted a 1.25% Net Smelter Returns royalty, with substantial advance payments contingent on mine construction and commercial production.

feed.title: Global $77 Billion Discrete Semiconductors Market Outlook, 2030 - Texas Instruments, ON Semiconductor, STMicroelectronics, Infineon Technologies, and NXP Semiconductors Dominate
feed.summary: The global discrete semiconductors market is projected to reach $77 billion by 2030, growing from $43.84 billion in 2025 at a CAGR of 11.9%. This growth is driven by increasing demand in consumer electronics, automotive, and industrial automation sectors, with key developments in high-efficiency MOSFETs and IGBTs. Leading companies like Texas Instruments, ON Semiconductor, and Infineon Technologies are focused on technological advancements and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: AMD (AMD) is Facing a Shortage That Can't Be Met, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted that Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is experiencing an insatiable demand for its products, leading to a shortage. This comment comes as AMD's shares have shown significant growth over the past year, with various financial firms like Citi, RBC Capital, Jefferies, and Truist maintaining positive ratings and price targets due to its AI business and prospects. Despite acknowledging AMD's potential, the article suggests that other AI stocks might offer greater returns with less risk.

feed.title: Chipotle brings back chicken al pastor amid social media push
feed.summary: Chipotle is reintroducing its popular chicken al pastor to its menu across multiple countries starting February 10, 2026, driven by significant customer demand on social media. This return marks an acceleration in Chipotle's menu innovation strategy for 2026, with plans for three to four limited-time protein offerings. The company notes that limited-time offerings increase customer frequency and spend, making them a key part of their engagement strategy.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Loves Intel (INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed strong approval for Intel (INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, praising his handling of the company's chip manufacturing and its new foundry business. Intel's shares have performed well, up 122% over the past year, with analysts citing solid demand and the potential of the Intel Foundry Business as key drivers. Cramer believes Intel is a "must-own" stock, despite some market share loss to AMD, attributing its success to Tan's leadership during product shortages.

feed.title: FedEx promotes longtime exec to C-suite
feed.summary: FedEx has promoted longtime executive Scott Ray to the C-suite as COO for U.S. and Canada operations. Ray has been with the company for nearly four decades and has played a crucial role in operational integrations that have reshaped FedEx's package movement strategies. This appointment marks his entry into FedEx's executive committee.

feed.title: Harmony Biosciences' Sales and Profits Have Soared. Why Hasn't Its Stock Followed?
feed.summary: Despite significant financial success with its narcolepsy drug Wakix, including rapidly rising sales and profits, Harmony Biosciences' stock price has remained largely flat since its IPO. This discrepancy is attributed to concerns over the patent cliff for Wakix in 2030, a damaging short-seller report in 2023 alleging "sham patents" and "scam clinical trials," and strong competition from companies like Jazz Pharmaceuticals. Shareholder lawsuits further contribute to the bearish sentiment, overshadowing the company's impressive financial performance.

feed.title: Starbucks Stock Has Soared in 2026. Is It Too Late to Buy?
feed.summary: Starbucks stock has risen 14% year-to-date in 2026, showing initial signs of a turnaround with improving comparable-store sales. Despite a decline in earnings and operating margin in fiscal Q4 2025, the company's Q4 revenue growth accelerated, and North America comparable-store sales stabilized. However, at 40 times forward earnings, the stock's current valuation might already price in a successful turnaround, leaving little margin for error for new investors, though it suggests holding for existing shareholders.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Reports Q4, 2025 Results
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp. reported net sales of $4.1 billion for the fourth quarter of 2025, a 0.6% decline from the previous year, and $16.4 billion for the full year. Despite the decline, organic sales growth was positive, and the company announced an increased quarterly dividend of $1.28 per share. Chairman and CEO Mike Hsu highlighted the company's "largest transformation," focusing on innovation, brand strength, cost discipline, and a pivot to higher-growth personal care categories.

feed.title: Liberty Broadband stock hits 52-week low at $42.77
feed.summary: Liberty Broadband Series C stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $42.77, marking a significant decline of over 40% in the past year. Despite its "GOOD" financial health and a low P/E ratio of 5.18, the stock continues to face downward pressure. Investors are awaiting the next earnings report on February 11, with analyst targets suggesting potential for undervaluation according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Liberty Broadband stock hits 52-week low at $42.68
feed.summary: Liberty Broadband Series A (LBRDA) stock has hit a new 52-week low of $42.68, reflecting a significant decline of over 40% in the past year. Despite its low P/E ratio, the company faces short-term liquidity issues as its current ratio is below 1. Investors are closely watching its upcoming earnings report on February 11th, with some analysis suggesting the stock may be undervalued despite recent poor financial performance.

feed.title: Piper Sandler sees Helios ramp and OpenAI driving AMD upside
feed.summary: Piper Sandler maintains an Overweight rating on AMD, forecasting significant upside driven by the ramp-up of the Helios cluster and increased demand from OpenAI. The firm expects AMD's AI-focused chips, particularly the MI300X, to see substantial growth, with a projected increase in AI revenue for 2025. This positive outlook is further supported by an anticipated market share gain against competitors like Nvidia.

feed.title: ONEOK chooses 2 new independent directors
feed.summary: ONEOK, Inc. has elected Mark A. McCollum and Precious Williams Owodunni as new independent directors to its board, effective January 23, 2026. These appointments aim to strengthen the board's expertise in energy operations, finance, corporate governance, and executive leadership. McCollum brings extensive experience from companies like Weatherford International and Halliburton, while Owodunni is the CEO of Mountaintop Consulting and has a background from Goldman Sachs.

feed.title: SEALSQ's Quantum Highway: Building Trust In Silicon For Global Connectivity
feed.summary: SEALSQ Corp (NASDAQ: LAES) has launched its "Quantum Highway" initiative, a fully integrated platform designed to embed cryptographic identity and post-quantum security directly into hardware. This leverages a vertically integrated approach, expanding from a pilot in Europe to global partnerships, ensuring secure connectivity across classical, post-quantum, and quantum environments. The strategy involves strategic acquisitions and investments to build a durable, hardware-rooted trust infrastructure for critical sectors.

feed.title: Analyst reaffirms HIVE outlook as miner posts strong Bitcoin growth
feed.summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods reaffirmed a "market perform" rating on HIVE Digital, sharply cutting its price target to $3.50 from $11, despite HIVE's strong operational performance. HIVE reported a 197% increase in Bitcoin production in December 2025 compared to the previous year, mining 306 BTC, and a 31% increase for the full year 2025, reaching 2,311 BTC. The company plans further expansion of its green energy data center capacity in Paraguay, while analyst sentiment on the stock remains mixed.

feed.title: The Williams Companies, Inc. Increases Regular Cash Dividend on Common Stock, Payable on March 30, 2026
feed.summary: The Williams Companies, Inc. announced a 5% increase in its regular quarterly cash dividend to $0.525 per share, or $2.10 annualized. This dividend will be payable on March 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 13, 2026. The increase reflects the company's commitment to returning value to shareholders.

feed.title: International Paper completes sale of global cellulose fibers business
feed.summary: International Paper has completed the sale of its global cellulose fibers (GCF) business unit to American Industrial Partners (AIP) for $1.5 billion. GCF, which produces fluff and specialty pulp, will now operate as an independent company headquartered in Memphis. The sale includes nine manufacturing facilities and 3,300 employees across the U.S., Canada, and Poland.

feed.title: Investor calls for CoStar board overhaul, exit from Homes.com
feed.summary: Activist investor Third Point is demanding an overhaul of CoStar Group's board and a withdrawal from its Homes.com platform, citing weak oversight, poor capital allocation, and significant value destruction. Third Point criticized CEO Andy Florance's compensation despite the company's weak stock performance and Homes.com's substantial losses. CoStar, however, maintains that its residential investment phase is winding down and analysts see potential in new AI-driven features for Homes.com.

feed.title: Teledyne Gavia Delivers Four GAVIA AUV Systems to the Swedish Defense Materiel Administration
feed.summary: Teledyne Gavia has successfully delivered the first four GAVIA Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUV) systems to the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration (FMV). This delivery is part of a multi-year framework agreement aimed at modernizing the Swedish Armed Forces' underwater surveillance capabilities. The modular GAVIA AUVs can be rapidly reconfigured for various missions, including mine countermeasures, hydrography, intelligence collection, seabed mapping, and surveillance.

feed.title: Jefferies Flags App Store and Google Ad Risks for Apple (AAPL)
feed.summary: Jefferies analyst Edison Lee lowered Apple's (AAPL) price target to $276.47, maintaining a Hold rating, due to concerns over slowing App Store growth and Google ad revenue risks. The firm noted a significant slowdown in App Store revenue growth and anticipates only a slight beat in Apple's upcoming results, keeping fiscal year 2026 and 2027 estimates in line with consensus. Despite Apple's potential, Jefferies suggests the stock will likely be range-bound given its PEG ratio of 2.4x.

feed.title: Union Pacific Q4 Results Fall Short as Revenue Slips, But Operations Improve - Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP)
feed.summary: Union Pacific (NYSE: UNP) reported disappointing Q4 2025 financial results, with net income and revenue falling short of analyst estimates. Despite the revenue slip and a worsening operating ratio, the company showed improvements in several operational metrics, including freight car velocity, terminal dwell, and productivity. Union Pacific also provided an outlook for high-single digit to low-double digit EPS growth through 2027 and a capital plan of $3.3 billion.

feed.title: Broadcom Set To Dominate Custom AI Chip Market With 60% Share By 2027, Counterpoint Says
feed.summary: Counterpoint Research predicts that Broadcom will lead the custom AI chip market, holding a 60% share by 2027, despite increasing competition. Hyperscalers like Google, Amazon, and Microsoft are rapidly scaling their internal AI server compute ASICs to meet surging demand. While Google's TPUs will remain foundational, the market is diversifying with significant contributions from Meta and Microsoft, shifting away from a duopoly.

feed.title: Lucid Announces Upcoming Investor Day
feed.summary: Lucid Group, Inc. announced it will host an Investor Day on March 12, 2026, to provide updates on its strategic priorities, operational execution, growth roadmap, and financial outlook. The event will feature presentations from senior leadership, an in-depth preview of its upcoming midsize vehicle program, and a live Q&A session. Additional details and webcast information will be available on Lucid's investor relations website.

feed.title: Alcoa downgraded at Morgan Stanley after strong YTD performance (AA:NYSE)
feed.summary: Alcoa (AA) was downgraded by Morgan Stanley to Equal Weight from Overweight, with a price target of $64. This downgrade comes despite Alcoa's 1.6% rise in Tuesday's trading and its strong year-to-date performance. Morgan Stanley believes the stock's risk-reward profile is now more balanced after it significantly outperformed its peers recently.

feed.title: TransDigm Group (TDG) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: TransDigm Group (TDG) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025, with an anticipated EPS of $8.02 and revenues of $2.25 billion. Analysts have recently become more bullish on the company, resulting in a positive Earnings ESP of +4.12%, suggesting a likely earnings beat. The company's strong Zacks Rank of #3 further supports this outlook, making it a compelling candidate for an earnings surprise.

feed.title: Safe Pro Group (NASDAQ: SPAI) Partners With Lantronix to Advance Edge AI Threat Detection for Defense Drones
feed.summary: Safe Pro Group (NASDAQ: SPAI) has partnered with Lantronix Inc. (NASDAQ: LTRX) to develop and integrate embeddable AI chipsets for threat detection in drones and autonomous vehicles. This collaboration aims to leverage Safe Pro's AI algorithms with Lantronix's Open-Q system-on-module solutions to enable real-time, on-device detection of threats like landmines, enhancing mission-critical performance without relying on cloud connectivity. The technology will be applied across defense, humanitarian, and homeland security sectors.

feed.title: Lantronix, Safe Pro Group Launch Partnership
feed.summary: Lantronix and Safe Pro Group have announced a new partnership to develop and commercialize embeddable chipsets for Qualcomm-based drone and autonomous vehicle platforms. This collaboration aims to integrate AI-enabled threat detection at the edge, strengthening the foundation for defense and autonomous systems globally. The agreement includes a memorandum of understanding and a master services agreement for joint development.

feed.title: Oak Valley Bancorp Announces Planned CEO Leadership Transition
feed.summary: Oak Valley Bancorp (OVLY) has announced that CEO Christopher M. Courtney will retire on June 30, 2026, and will be succeeded by current President and COO Richard A. McCarty. Courtney led the bank's growth from two branches and $45 million in assets to 19 branches and over $2 billion in assets. The transition is part of a planned strategy to maintain the bank's credit quality, community focus, and growth trajectory.

feed.title: Apple sued for allegedly breaking US antitrust law, stealing camera tech
feed.summary: Reincubate Ltd, makers of the smartphone video app Camo, has sued Apple for allegedly stealing its patented technology and maintaining an illegal monopoly in the U.S. smartphone software market. The lawsuit claims Apple copied Camo's features into its iOS "Continuity Camera" after actively encouraging Reincubate's development, thereby stifling competition. This action follows a similar antitrust lawsuit filed by the U.S. government against Apple in 2024.

feed.title: Why UnitedHealth Group Stock Just Crashed
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group's stock crashed by 19% after its Q4 2025 earnings report. While adjusted earnings met expectations, GAAP earnings plummeted by nearly 100% year-over-year, from $5.98 to $0.01 per share. Despite the negative Q4 GAAP results, the company forecasts a rebound in GAAP earnings for 2026, potentially making the current sell-off a buying opportunity.

feed.title: Eli Lilly stock edges up as Roche obesity-drug data keeps weight-loss race in view
feed.summary: Eli Lilly shares saw a modest increase amidst a weaker healthcare sector, driven by new data from Roche's Phase II obesity drug, CT-388, which showed promising weight loss results. This intensified focus on the competitive obesity drug market ahead of Lilly's upcoming earnings report on February 4th. Investors are keenly awaiting Lilly's results to assess demand, supply, and pricing for its own weight-loss drugs, Mounjaro and Zepbound, as new challengers emerge and the long-term impact of AI in drug development is still uncertain.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Q4 Earnings Top Estimates, Organic Sales Up 2.1%
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) reported Q4 2025 results, with adjusted earnings surpassing Zacks Consensus Estimate due to increased operating profit and reduced diluted shares. While the top line missed expectations with a slight decline, organic sales grew by 2.1%, driven by volume and mix expansion. The company provided optimistic guidance for 2026, including anticipated organic sales growth and double-digit adjusted EPS growth on a constant-currency basis, leveraging productivity savings and strategic acquisitions.
